metadata.title:
ALL BUT


THREE BUTTE


MINES CLOSE
SUSPEND BECAUSE ENGINEERS
DESIRE TO SEPARATE FROM


WESTERN FEDERATION.
5000 MEN ARE IDLE

5000 MEN ARE IDLE

pages.raw_text:
(By United Press Leased Wire.)


BUTTE, Mont, Feb. 16.-Every
mine In and around Butte, with the
exception Of the Silver Bow, the
Berkeley and the Mountain Consol.
idated, suspended operations today.


The suspension was the result OF
an attempt on the part Of the
union engineers employed at the
mines to separate from the Western
Federation Of Miners and organize
union to affiliate with the Inter.
national Engineers union.


1t Is estimated today that 5000
men are idle.


The smelling works of former
Senator w. A. Clark closed this
morning. According to General
Manager John Glllie, OF the Anal
gamated Copper company, the
Amalgamated Copper company, the
Washoe smelters at Anaconda. en-
ploying 5000 men, the Boston &
Montana smelters at Great Falls,
employing 1000 men. probably will
have to close within four days.


The, mines in this city which have
not closed all belong to the Anal
gamated company. They are run
ming under difficulty with crippled
forces.


The direct cause of the suspen-
sion was the refusal of the en-
gineers to go to work this morning.
